summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc integration in Firefox have enhanced how the browser handles video streaming and communication via WebRTC, the technology behind video calls. Notably, improvements have been made to the integration of H.264 video format support, which is widely used for its efficiency in compressing video. This includes the implementation of an H.264 FMTP (Format Parameter) parser, which helps the browser more accurately determine the capabilities of video streams based on their resolution and framerate. With these changes, Firefox can better manage video quality and performance, creating a smoother experience for users without any visible changes in the current interface. Furthermore, logging improvements through the *MOZ_LOG_FMT* will assist developers in diagnosing issues more effectively, although this will not impact end-user experience directly. Overall, these enhancements are significant for improving video call quality and ensuring compatibility with various media formats.
